chapters.summary: Council staff calls the meeting to order and welcomes attendees to the New York City Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education vote.
chapters.title: Call to order and welcome to Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: You got this paper.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Good morning.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Welcome to the New York City Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Please do not approach the days.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair, we are ready to begin.

chapters.summary: Chair Jennifer Gutiérrez convenes the vote, acknowledging present colleagues including Councilmembers Joseph, Brewer, Felder, and Riley online.
chapters.title: Chair Gutiérrez opens vote and acknowledges committee members

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Good morning, everyone.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I'm Councilmember Jennifer Gutierrez, Chair of the Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Welcome to today's vote.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: First, I want to acknowledge my colleagues who are present for the committee, Councilmember Forever Chair Rita Joseph, Councilmember Gail Brewer, Councilmember Simcha Felder, and online we have Councilmember Riley and Councilmember Juan.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Yes?

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Okay, I take it back.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Just Councilmember Riley.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez describes a landmark moment for universal child care, citing recent state-level funding boosts and buy-in across government levels as an opportunity to transform children's and families' lives.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez frames defining moment for universal child care in NYC

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Today, the subcommittee will consider a package of legislation geared towards improving New York's early childhood education system.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: We have reached a defining moment in the movement for universal child care in New York City.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: A few weeks ago, the state legislature made a landmark investment in child care.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The boost in early childhood education funding, plus the buy-in from leadership across all levels of New York government, has created an opportunity to truly transform the lives of children and families.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: But we need to ensure that the system...

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez summarizes the subcommittee's recent hearings with providers, families, and advocates, noting the legislation being voted on addresses issues raised and prepares the system for future growth.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez reviews prior hearings on early childhood education system issues

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: We are expanding upon is strong.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Over the course of the last several months, the subcommittee has held hearings where we've heard from providers, families, and advocates about a range of issues within the New York City early childhood education system.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Welcome to Deputy Speaker Nantasha Williams, who's joining us.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Let's go next.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The legislation we're considering today takes an important first step at addressing some of those issues and preparing the system for future growth.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez describes her sponsored bill requiring DOE to run an annual outreach and education campaign about early childhood education programs, covering benefits, options, application processes, and available seats to boost enrollment.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez presents Intro 579A: outreach campaign for early childhood education enrollment

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I'm proud to be the lead sponsor of one of the bills the subcommittee will be considering, proposed Intro 579A,

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: in relation to an education and outreach campaign to enroll students in early childhood education programs would require the Department of Education to develop and implement an annual outreach and education campaign regarding early childhood education programs.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The outreach would include information on the benefits of participating in early childhood education, the options for early childhood education, the application process, and the resources to find programs that may have available seats.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: This year, roughly the same number of New York City parents applied to preschool programs as they did last year.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: We need to ensure that we're reaching all families about the early childhood education options available to them.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: This bill aims to engage more families about those options and boost enrollment in early childhood education programs.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez explains Councilmember Linda Lee's bill requiring the Mayor's Office of Child Care to study DOHMH permitting and state licensing processes, report on overlaps and contradictions, and recommend streamlining.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez presents Intro 9A: study on child care permitting and licensing processes

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The subcommittee will also be considering proposed Intro 9A sponsored by Councilmember Linda Lee in relation to a study and report on the processes for obtaining permits and licenses or registrations to operate a child care program.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: This bill would require the Mayor's Office of Child Care to continue its work on reviewing child care holistically by studying the process to get a permit from DOHMH and to get a license or registration from the New York State Office of Child and Family Services.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: This bill would require...

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: a report on the office's study of those processes and shine a light on any overlaps and contradictions and provide recommendations for streamlining.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez introduces Deputy Speaker Williams's resolution calling on the state legislature to amend workers' compensation law to allow unused paid family leave to transfer to grandparents and other designated caregivers.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez presents Resolution 165: paid family leave transfer to grandparents

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The subcommittee will also consider two resolutions that call on the New York State Legislature to pass legislation in relation to child care.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Resolution 165, sponsored by our Deputy Speaker, calls on the New York State Legislature to introduce and pass and the Governor to sign legislation to amend the workers'compensation law in relation to allowing unused paid family leave

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: to be transferred to grandparents and other designated caregivers.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez presents Councilmember Riley's resolution urging the state to pass Senate Bill 1994 and Assembly Bill 2218, which would remove minimum wage and hours requirements for child care assistance applicants.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez presents Resolution 365A: removing minimum wage requirements for child care assistance

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Proposed Res.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: 365A, sponsored by Councilmember Riley, calls on the New York State Legislature to pass and the Governor to sign

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Senate Bill 1994, Assembly Bill 2218, to remove the minimum wage and hours requirements for applicants of child care assistance.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: These state bills make small, important changes that impact the ability of families in accessing child care supports.

chapters.summary: Chair Gutiérrez closes her opening remarks by encouraging colleagues to support the package, thanking subcommittee and personal staff by name, and acknowledging her baby Lulu.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez urges support for legislative package and thanks subcommittee staff

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: All of the legislation we've considered today responds to everyday challenges people face when interacting with the early childhood education system in New York City.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I encourage my fellow committee members to support this legislative package.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Today we can make small improvements to one of our city's most foundational systems.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Finally, I would like to thank the subcommittee staff, Julia Goldsmith-Pinkham, Katie Selena, Grace Amato, Andrew Lane Lawless, and Margaret Barnsley, as well as my own staff, Frank Hulka and El Alau for their work to prepare this meeting, and to my baby, Lulu.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Clerk, we are ready to...

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Begin.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Oh, and pause.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Oh, okay, I get to read more.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Okay.

chapters.summary: On behalf of Councilmember Lee, Chair Gutiérrez reads a statement describing how child care is impossible to find for many families, with providers stuck in paperwork and families losing income when care is unavailable.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez reads Councilmember Lee's statement on the child care access crisis

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Sorry.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: It's okay.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I'm going to read this on behalf of Councilmember Lee, who's passing legislation today regarding Intro 9A.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: For too many families in New York City, child care is not just expensive, it is impossible to find.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Parents are ready to go back to work but cannot find a safe, reliable place for their child.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Providers are ready to serve more families but are forced to spend months navigating paperwork, inspections, approvals, and unclear agency processes.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: That is time families do not have.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: When a parent cannot find child care, a whole household is affected.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: A mother turns down a shift.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: A father misses work.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: A family loses income.

chapters.summary: Continuing Lee's statement, Gutiérrez explains that Intro 9A does not weaken safety standards but makes rules clear and coordinated, identifies delays, and builds a permitting process that protects children without blocking access to care.
chapters.title: Gutiérrez reads Lee's statement: how Intro 9A addresses permitting barriers

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: A child loses the chance to begin learning, playing, and growing in a safe early childhood setting.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Intro 9A is about taking that problem seriously.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: The bill does not weaken health and safety standards.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: It does the opposite.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: It says that safety rules should be clear, coordinated, and workable.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Providers should know what is required, which agency is responsible, and how long each step should take.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: If we want universal child care to become real, we cannot allow good providers to get stuck in a maze before they even open their doors.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: New York City needs more child care seats, but we also need a system that helps create those seats responsibly, quickly, and fairly.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Intro 9A is a practical step towards that goal.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: It will help us identify the delays, listen to providers and families, and build a permitting process that protects children without blocking access to care.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I'm grateful to me, Speaker Menon, and all the advocates, providers, and families who have helped move this bill forward.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Congrats, Councilmember Lee.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Okay, we're ready for a vote, friend.

chapters.summary: Committee Clerk William Martin conducts a roll call vote on all coupled items. Chair Gutiérrez, Councilmembers Riley, Brewer, Joseph, and Felder vote aye, with Brewer noting concerns about burdens on providers.
chapters.title: Roll call vote on all early childhood education items

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Good morning.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: William Martin, Committee Clerk, Roll Call Vote Committee on Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: All items are coupled.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Chair Gutierrez.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Proudly aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Riley.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Brewer.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I do think that the...

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Ready, so I always worry about burdens having worked for many of them in the past, but I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Joseph.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: One.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Felder.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: Can you vote?

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: And then we'll ask the question later.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Yes, on all.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: The clerk announces a vote of five in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions. All items in the early childhood education legislative package are approved by the subcommittee.
chapters.title: All items approved by subcommittee

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Santos also.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: With a vote of five in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions, all items have been approved by the subcommittee.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: We're all done.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

summary: The NYC Council Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education votes on a package of legislation including Intro 579A (outreach campaign), Intro 9A (permitting study), and two state-level resolutions on paid family leave and child care assistance eligibility.
Chair Jennifer Gutiérrez frames the vote as a defining moment for universal child care, citing recent state funding boosts and prior subcommittee hearings with providers, families, and advocates.
All items pass with a 5-0 vote, with Councilmember Brewer expressing cautious support and concerns about provider burdens.
title: Subcommittee on Early Childhood Education
